title: dianthus petraeus subsp. minutiflorus
intro: Dianthus petraeus subsp. minutiflorus, also known as the small-flowered rock pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and protecting against infections. The health benefits of Dianthus petraeus subsp. minutiflorus include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making it a potential natural remedy for minor cuts and scrapes. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infused in oil to be used topically for its medicinal properties.
